events.name: District Professional Learning / Chavez Huerta Day

calendarNotes:
Fort Worth ISD begins the 2025-2026 school year on August 12, 2025 — one of the earliest start dates among Texas's large urban districts. Spring Break runs March 16–20, 2025, aligned with neighboring Dallas ISD. The school year ends May 21, 2025 — about a week earlier than most Texas and national peers. Fort Worth's earlier last day reflects fewer total instructional days (175) than districts like Dallas ISD or Houston ISD (180). Families moving to Fort Worth from other states should note the significantly earlier summer compared to districts that run into early June.

Fort Worth ISD and Dallas ISD are separate school districts despite being part of the same DFW metropolitan area. They serve different cities, are governed by independent school boards, and set their own academic calendars. While their Spring Break windows often align within a day or two, start and end dates can differ. Families with children in both districts — or moving between the two — should treat their calendars as independent and verify dates on each district's official website rather than assuming DFW uniformity.

Fort Worth ISD observes Good Friday (April 3) as a school holiday — a holiday not universally observed by all Texas districts and generally unfamiliar to families from states that do not observe it. In 2025-2026, Good Friday falls one week after Spring Break ends (March 22 return), giving families a short school week before the Easter weekend. Families relocating from the Northeast or Pacific Coast should note this Texas-common holiday on the calendar.

Fort Worth ISD has expanded its Early College High School and Career and Technical Education (CTE) programs. Students enrolled in ECHS programs take dual-enrollment college courses through Tarrant County College and may have additional required testing or orientation days not reflected in the standard district calendar. Contact your child's campus directly for ECHS-specific scheduling requirements.
